channel.title: HSJ - Latest news
channel.description: Custom RSS feed for HSJ latest news

item.title: Exclusive: Trust pleads guilty after patient fire death
item.description: A trust has pleaded guilty to fire safety offences relating to a patient’s death in a rare case where a fire service has brought a prosecution against an NHS provider, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: ‘Operational efficiency’ AI tools part of new £900m procurement push
item.description: AI systems designed to reduce costs and improve outcomes through “workflow automation” have been included in an NHS procurement framework worth up to £900m.

item.title: Trust sued by staff over ‘hazardous’ exposure to gas
item.description: Dozens of staff members working in a major hospital trust’s maternity unit are seeking compensation over claims they were exposed to “hazardous” levels of gas and air.

item.title: New CEO for trust recovering from abuse scandal
item.description: A mental health trust that has been battling to recover from an abuse scandal has appointed a new chief executive.

item.title: Revealed: ‘Catastrophic’ gaps in tech regulation plans
item.description: Proposed amendments to UK medical device regulations are “a disgrace” and risk creating the lowest barrier to entry for high-risk AI devices in the developed world, sector experts have told HSJ.

item.title: Mapped: RTT waiting times
item.description: An interactive map of local NHS waits around England in March 2026, showing the pressures, with links to all the details by organisation and specialty.

item.title: Controversial group model could be scrapped
item.description: Two trusts that formed a controversial group model are launching a review, which could lead to the partnership being split up.

item.title: Single patient record could save £110m, says DHSC
item.description: The new single patient record will save £110m over the next 10 years, according to an impact assessment accompanying publication of the NHS bill.

item.title: Exclusive: Southport attack victim accuses trust of ‘cover up’ over care records breach
item.description: Hospital staff inappropriately accessed the medical records of victims of the 2024 mass stabbing at a dance class in Southport, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: Ministers take powers to overrule ‘unresponsive ICBs’
item.description: The government is taking a raft of new powers over the NHS – including one that will enable them to direct integrated care boards on virtually any of their functions – in its new Health Bill, published on Thursday.

item.title: New health secretary appointed
item.description: The prime minister has appointed a new health and social care secretary, following Wes Streeting’s resignation.

item.title: Increase in corridor care ‘haemorrhaging morale’, trust told
item.description: Governors at one of the largest trusts in the country have warned that moving patients from beds to chairs to free up space is a risk to staff and public morale.

item.title: Streeting quits government
item.description: Wes Streeting has resigned as health and social care secretary.

item.title: Elective target hit after spike in ‘unreported removals’
item.description: NHS England has hit its 65 per cent waiting list for 2025-26, but experts said the “majority of the improvement” in March was driven by a “record” spike in “unreported removals”.

item.title: NHSE lead to stand in as CQC chief inspector
item.description: NHS England’s first medical director for mental health and neurodiversity is being seconded to the Care Quality Commission for six months.

item.title: NHS churned through 48 ministers in 11 years
item.description: Forty-eight people have served as ministers in the Department of Health and Social Care in the 11 years since the 2015 general election, with an average tenure of just 487 days, according to analysis by HSJ.

item.title: Major trust names new chair after axing shared model
item.description: A major trust has named its new chair after ending a shared agreement with its neighbour.

item.title: Trusts expect to miss emergency care target
item.description: Four of England’s 10 ambulance trusts are expecting to miss the headline response time target for 2026-27, according to their plans for the year.

item.title: New health minister appointed
item.description: Britain’s first female Sikh MP has been appointed as a health minister.

item.title: Revealed: Hospitals with the highest avoidable admissions
item.description: Around 15 per cent of emergency admissions at some trusts are potentially avoidable, according to new NHS England data.
